title: TrueNAS SCALE ZFS pool vdev degraded faulted or removed disk state
dataSources: TrueNAS REST pool status indexed as `truenas:pool`
app: Custom scripted input or TrueNAS community TA; HEC from `/api/v2.0/pool`
spl:
index=storage sourcetype="truenas:pool" earliest=-15m
| eval st=coalesce(status, state, health)
| search degraded OR faulted OR removed OR offline OR UNAVAIL
| eval pool=coalesce(pool_name, name)
| table _time, hostname, pool, st, scan_state, topology_summary
description: Degraded vdevs mean single-disk failure away from pool loss. Immediate visibility into `FAULTED` or `REMOVED` topology nodes drives faster disk swap and sparing decisions.
value: Protects NAS and VM stores from silent escalation to unrecoverable RAID-Z loss.
implementation: Poll `/api/v2.0/pool` every 60s during incidents, 5 minutes steady state. Map `topology` JSON to a short `topology_summary` field at ingest. Authenticate with API keys in `passwords.conf`.
knownFalsePositives: Short spikes during approved changes, maintenance windows, or known batch jobs can match the rule; confirm against the vendor console and change calendar.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see how object storage is growing, who can reach it, and when something is exposed or mis-tagged, so cloud storage stays under control.
